body:
Ethan,

Following up on Benny's walk from this morning — he came back with mud up to his elbows and I found a stick the size of a small branch on the kitchen floor, so I assume the 30 minutes was well spent.

I've got a vendor call with Marco at 9 and the Hartwell kickoff right after, so I won't be around to do the evening loop. Can you take him out around 5:30? Stick to the Rittenhouse route — he's been pulling toward the construction zone on 21st and I'd rather not deal with that.

Appreciate it.
Dad

Dad,

Yeah, the stick situation was a whole thing. He found it about four minutes in and refused to put it down the entire walk. I didn't fight it.

5:30 works. I'll do the Rittenhouse loop and keep him away from 21st. Should I feed him before or after? I can never remember if you do it before the walk or wait until after.

Also — Sophie mentioned she might stop by around 6. Just a heads-up in case you're still on calls.

Ethan

Hi Ethan,

Following up on case #48821 from last week. I ran the descaling cycle as advised — twice — and the Vertuo Plus is still pulling short on the lungo. Odacio pods are coming out around 3.5 oz instead of the full 5. Machine is about 18 months old.

I use this every morning before I'm out the door, so it's not a casual inconvenience. If we're heading toward a replacement rather than another troubleshooting round, I'd rather know that now.

Mark Campbell
Campbell Events

Hi Mark,

Thank you for following up and for running through the descaling steps — that rules out the most common cause.

Under-extraction that persists after two descaling cycles at 18 months typically points to a pump pressure issue. I've flagged your case to our technical team as a priority escalation. You should hear from them within 24–48 hours with an assessment — if the unit is outside repair scope, we'll move straight to replacement under your registration.

Apologies for the disruption to your morning routine. We'll get this resolved as quickly as we can.

Ethan Campbell
Nespresso Customer Care

Ethan,

Technical team reached out this morning — replacement unit confirmed, which I appreciate. Straightforward process.

Only issue: it ships Monday, arriving Wednesday at the earliest. My lungo with oat milk is a non-negotiable start to the day, so I'll be making do at the coffee shop until it lands. Any chance to push that to expedited shipping? Even a day's difference helps.

If Monday is the hard floor, understood — just wanted to ask before accepting the timeline.

Mark Campbell

Ethan,

Following up on the charge from Tuesday. I ordered my usual — oat milk latte, large — and the app billed me twice. I sent a screenshot to the support inbox last Thursday with both transaction IDs. Haven't heard back.

I'm in most Wednesday mornings before 8:30, so I'd rather sort this before it becomes a pattern. Can you confirm it's been flagged on your end?

Also, whoever made it Tuesday got the ratio right. First time in about two weeks. Just noting it.

— Mark

Hi Dad,

Found the ticket — it was sitting in the secondary queue, which is on us. The duplicate charge has been reversed. You should see it post within 3–5 business days depending on your bank. I've added a note to your account so the team knows to watch for it going forward.

I'll pass the feedback along on the latte. Marcus made it Tuesday — he's newer but he actually adjusts when you tell him something. No guarantees on consistency, but I'll say something.

— Ethan

Ethan,

Good. Three to five days is fine, I just needed it confirmed rather than sitting open.

On Marcus — is he on Wednesday mornings? I'm usually there around 8:15 before my weekly kickoff with Becca. It'd be useful to have a consistent person rather than recalibrating every other visit. The oat milk latte is a small thing but it's the start of the day and I'd rather it land correctly.

Let me know his usual shift.

— Mark

Hi Dad,

Marcus is Wednesday 7–11, so you'll catch him. I put your usual in the system under your name — large oat milk latte, standard pull. Should just be a name confirmation at the counter and he'll know.

Refund cleared on my end this morning, so you're all set. Come in and tell me if it's still off.

— Ethan

Ethan,

Becca and I have our weekly kickoff Monday morning and I want the milestone tracker current before we sit down. Last I checked, the Hargrove wedding on the 28th still had vendor confirmation and final headcount flagged as pending — can you pull that up and confirm whether either of those got resolved?

Also need the catering notes from Marco's last email on the Delgado event. I didn't forward it before I left Friday. Check the shared inbox — subject line should include "Delgado November."

If anything's still open by Sunday night, flag it. I'd rather handle it before the meeting than during it.

Dad

Dad,

Checked the tracker. Hargrove vendor confirmation came through Thursday — I updated the sheet. Headcount is still pending; the client hasn't responded to Becca's follow-up from Wednesday. She noted she'd call them Monday morning before your kickoff, so you should have it before you sit down.

Found the Delgado catering notes. Marco sent two emails — one on the 12th with the draft menu, one on the 14th with pricing. I've forwarded both and flagged them in the shared inbox. The plated dinner option came in under budget, for what it's worth.

Hargrove headcount is the only live item. Otherwise you're in good shape for Monday.

Ethan

Ethan,

Following up on the Gmail API issue we talked about last week. Becca still can't pull client emails into the dashboard — it's been breaking her pre-kickoff prep, which means I'm going into Monday reviews without a full picture of where things stand.

I know you said the OAuth scope might need to be updated. Has that been addressed, or is it still sitting in the queue? If it's a quick fix, I'd rather get it done before this weekend — we have three weddings running concurrently and the coordination volume is going to be high.

Let me know what you need from my end.

Dad

Dad,

Apologies for the delay — I had it flagged but it slipped behind a few other tickets. I pushed it to the top today.

The issue was the OAuth token expiring without a proper refresh flow in place. I've updated the scopes to include gmail.readonly and gmail.send, and added token auto-refresh. Becca should be able to re-authenticate through the settings page and it'll stay connected going forward. I'll send her a quick note with the steps.

One thing to be aware of: the re-auth will ask for permissions again. It's standard, nothing alarming — just click through. Should take her under two minutes.

Let me know if it doesn't work after she tries it.

Ethan

Ethan,

Becca just confirmed it's working — she tested it before our kickoff this morning and the inbox sync came through clean. Good timing.

For what it's worth, this is the second time an OAuth token has quietly expired without any kind of alert. Worth building in a notification so we're not finding out when something breaks. Something to flag for the next sprint, not urgent.

Thanks for getting on it.

Dad

Hi Ethan,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last week — the Google Calendar API sync issue I reported. Still seeing the same problem: events created through our booking platform aren't pushing to the shared Campbell Events calendar that Becca and I both use to track client milestones.

This is the calendar we rely on to manage all active wedding timelines. Right now Becca is manually cross-checking everything before our Monday kickoffs, which is a workaround, not a fix. We have six weddings in the next eight weeks and the coordination overhead compounds fast.

Can you confirm whether there's been any update on the OAuth scope issue your team flagged? Last note I have is from December 11 saying you were escalating internally.

Mark Campbell
Campbell Events, Philadelphia

Hi Mark,

Thanks for following up — I apologize for the gap in communication on our end.

I checked with our integrations team this morning. The root cause is a scope mismatch introduced in a credential refresh on December 9. Your connected app lost write access to the shared calendar while retaining read access, which is why events appear to sync but don't actually land. The fix requires reauthorizing the integration under the correct OAuth scopes (calendar.events + calendar).

I can walk you through the reauthorization steps now, or if you'd prefer, I can set up a 15-minute screen share with you and Becca this week. Either way, the fix itself is straightforward — under 10 minutes once we're in the right settings panel.

Let me know what works best.

Ethan Campbell
Technical Support

Ethan,

That explains it — thanks for digging into it.

Becca and I are in a kickoff Monday morning, so a screen share before 9 AM ET or anytime Tuesday works. If neither fits your schedule, just send the reauthorization steps and I'll run through it myself. I've done OAuth reconnects before; I just want to make sure I'm hitting the right scopes and not creating a second misconfigured connection.

Also worth noting: once this is resolved, I'd like to confirm that recurring events we set up for multi-day venue installations also sync correctly. We have a setup running Wednesday through Friday for a Saturday wedding at Terrain and I need those blocks visible on both ends.

Mark

Hi Mark,

Tuesday at 2 PM ET works on my end — I'll send a calendar invite shortly.

In the meantime, here are the reauthorization steps if you want to get ahead of it: (1) Go to your platform's Integrations settings and disconnect the Google Calendar connection entirely. (2) Reconnect using the same Google account, and when the OAuth consent screen appears, make sure both 'See, edit, share, and permanently delete all calendars' and 'See and edit events on all your calendars' are checked. (3) Save and trigger a manual sync.

For recurring events: yes, once the scopes are corrected, multi-day recurring blocks will sync in full including Wednesday–Friday setup days. That Terrain window should populate cleanly.

If you run into anything before Tuesday, reply here and I'll pick it up same day.

Ethan Campbell
Technical Support

Ethan,

Following up on the Notion API issue from last week. We're still hitting the same authentication failure when pulling vendor contracts from the shared Campbell Events drive. Token refresh isn't firing — requests time out around the 45-minute mark, which is exactly what I described before.

Becca flagged it again during our kickoff this morning. We've got three weddings loading in over the next two weeks, and she needs consistent folder access before Monday. Right now she's working around it manually, which is not a sustainable fix.

Last we spoke, the outstanding item was updating the service account permissions. Can you confirm whether that was done, and if not, what the current status is?

Mark

Hi Mark,

Apologies for the delay — I should have closed the loop sooner.

The service account permissions were updated yesterday afternoon. Root cause was a missing drive.readonly scope in the OAuth config, which caused token refresh to fail silently after the initial session expired. That explained the 45-minute cutoff.

Fix is deployed. To pick up the corrected token, someone will need to re-authenticate once: Settings → Integrations → Notion → Reconnect. Takes about 30 seconds. After that, refresh should run automatically on schedule with no interruption.

Happy to walk Becca through it if that's easier, or jump on a quick call this afternoon if anything looks off after reconnecting.

Ethan

Hi Ethan,

Following up on the WhatsApp API integration we discussed last week. We're trying to get it connected to the client communication workflow here at Campbell Events — the goal is automating reminder messages to couples in the 30-day lead-up to their wedding. Right now Becca's sending those manually and it's eating time she doesn't have.

I submitted the business verification through Meta on the 11th and still haven't seen an approval or a rejection. Can you tell me where that stands and what the typical turnaround looks like?

Also need to confirm: once approved, does the API sandbox environment support template message testing before we go live? Want to make sure we're not sending garbage to actual clients.

Mark Campbell
Campbell Events, Philadelphia

Hi Mark,

Thanks for the follow-up. I checked your account — the business verification is sitting in Meta's review queue and is showing as under manual review, which typically adds 3–5 business days on top of the standard window. Based on your submission date of the 11th, you should expect a decision by end of next week at the latest. I'll flag it on our end to expedite if it stalls past Friday.

On your sandbox question: yes, the sandbox fully supports template message testing. You'll be able to send test messages to up to five verified recipient numbers before your number goes live. I'd recommend getting your 30-day reminder templates pre-approved in parallel so you're ready to deploy immediately after verification clears.

I'll send over the template submission guide separately — it'll save you a round trip once you're approved.

Ethan Campbell
Customer Support

Ethan,

Good to know — flagging it if it stalls past Friday is appreciated. We have a busy stretch coming up and I'd like this live before the end of the month if possible.

The sandbox clarification helps. I'll have Becca pull together the reminder templates this week so we can submit them in parallel like you suggested. If the format has specific character limits or variable restrictions I should know about before she drafts them, include that in the guide.

One last thing: once we're live, is there a dashboard where I can see delivery and read receipts per message, or is that only available through API queries? Becca will be managing the day-to-day and she's not going to be running API calls.

Thanks for staying on top of this.

Mark Campbell
Campbell Events, Philadelphia
